entry.title: BIP 118 and SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
entry.summary:
The concept of package relay in Bitcoin Core is a sophisticated idea that extends beyond simple fee calculation to include the capability of transaction modification before they are processed by a local bitcoind. The implementation of SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT, for instance, requires a network that not only understands complex fee structures but can also perform specific modifications on transactions. This would involve the ability to handle transactions in such a way that an incoming transaction can declare its readiness to be linked with any prior unspent transaction output (UTXO) from a specified index.
This process demands that the receiving node must identify the relevant transaction in its mempool or blockchain that spends the specified UTXO and correctly link this to the incoming transaction's input. Following this, the modified transaction, now complete with the necessary details filled in, would be forwarded to the node’s local bitcoind for final processing. Such advancements in transaction handling could significantly enhance the efficiency and flexibility of the Bitcoin network, allowing for more complex transaction types and smarter processing protocols.
